title: War Games and Warnings on Strait of Hormuz Went Unheeded by Trump
summary: Over the past two decades, Iran repeatedly threatened to close down the waterway. President Trump underestimated Iran’s ability to do so.

title: Trump’s Controversial Pick to Oversee U.S. Intelligence, and a Surge in Preventable Diseases
summary: Plus, how Marcia Lucas was “the heart” behind “Star Wars.”

title: A Trump Endorsement Falls Flat: 4 Election Takeaways From Iowa and Beyond
summary: The president’s pick for governor of Iowa lost his primary, while Democrats in the state chose their nominee in what they hope will be a competitive Senate race.

title: 5 Takeaways From the California Primary Election
summary: Without a dominant candidate in the governor’s race, Democratic voters ultimately wanted to ensure that their party wasn’t shut out of the general election.

title: Trump Aims New Tariffs at 59 Countries and the European Union
summary: Trump administration officials said they planned to impose levies of up to 12.5 percent on countries that failed to crack down on goods made with forced labor.

title: New Intelligence Role Puts Bill Pulte’s Housing Agenda in Doubt
summary: Mr. Pulte has had difficulty boosting the housing market as head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency. Now he will also serve as acting intelligence director.

title: Order Shielding Trump Family From I.R.S. Audits Will Remain, Blanche Says
summary: The acting attorney general said the administration was preserving a broad order protecting the president and his family from audits of already filed returns, despite dropping a $1.8 billion payout fund.

title: Justice Dept. Won’t Proceed With Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund, Blanche Says
summary: The acting attorney general told lawmakers that he would leave in place an order forcing the I.R.S. to drop investigations into President Trump, his family and his businesses.

title: Trump Suggests Vance and Rubio Should Run Together in 2028 Election
summary: President Trump said Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio should split a ticket, without mentioning who should be at the top.

title: Want Courtside Seats for the Knicks? Be Rich, Famous and a Die-Hard Fan.
summary: Prime tickets on the floor for the N.B.A. Finals are coveted and very hard to get. The No. 1 requirement: You must love the Knicks.

title: Ahead of NBA Finals, NYC Reverses Ban on Knicks Watch Party Outside Madison Square Garden
summary: The decision came on Wednesday as the Knicks prepared for Game 1 of their series against the Spurs for the N.B.A. title.

title: Bari Weiss Speaks on Scott Pelley’s ’60 Minutes’ Firing: ‘That’s the Path That He Chose’
summary: In her first public comments on the firing, Ms. Weiss, the CBS News editor in chief, said that the longtime correspondent had “broken” the trust in the newsroom.

title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’
summary: Mr. Pelley, a former “CBS Evening News” anchor, was ousted after months of tensions between staff and Bari Weiss, the network’s editor in chief.

title: Only the Right Tests Can Stop This Ebola Outbreak. Congo Has Hardly Any.
summary: A chronic lack of investment in development of better tests has left clinicians blind and allows deadly viruses to spread unchecked.

title: At Interlochen, Where Jeffrey Epstein’s Shadow Still Lingers
summary: The arts school and camp is still contending with the fallout from its former ties to Mr. Epstein, an alumnus and donor accused of preying on two girls he met there.

title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
summary: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.

title: This Rugged New York Heron Is Suddenly Facing Extinction in the City. Why?
summary: Black-crowned night herons could go the way of the passenger pigeon, vanishing from the city. Why remains a mystery.

title: How Taylor Swift Writes a Song, In Her Own Words
summary: The artist shares stories behind some of her biggest hits, her love of a “rant bridge” and how life in the public eye informs the stories she tells in her songs.

title: America Broke Something When It Gave Trump a Second Chance
summary: Lincoln would know how to deal with these Republicans.

title: Feeling Nihilistic? You’re Not Alone.
summary: A case for mourning the American dream.

title: Painted Into a Corner
summary: We look at the state of the Iran war three months on.

title: Late Night Wants Trump to Give Peace Talks a Chance
summary: “Oh, I’m sorry, is wrapping up the war you started not interesting enough?” Michael Kosta said on Tuesday, after the president called negotiations with Iran “boring.”

title: FBI Kills Suspect in Hostage Situation at Building With Bank in Bakersfield, California
summary: The authorities had been negotiating with a man inside an office building in Bakersfield, Calif., that included a Chase Bank branch. It was unclear how many people he held hostage.
